🔍 THREAT HUNT  |  🛡️ WAZUH SIEM/EDR  |  🚨 SURICATA IPS  |  🔥 ACTIVE RESPONSE

Team View:
Ramiz Alsafi  ·  Generated: 2026-02-24 12:13
Database: 2,128 threats

🔴 Red Team Intelligence — Attack Perspective

💥 High-Value Exploit Chains

  • Filter P1 + EXPLOIT badge → prioritize initial access vectors
  • Cross-reference KEV list with EPSS >70% for active weaponization
  • ZDI advisories indicate unpublished or limited-patch PoC availability
  • Chain: Initial Access → Execution → Persistence → Priv Esc → Impact

🗺️ Kill Chain Mapping

  • TA0001 Initial Access — phishing, supply chain, public-facing apps
  • TA0002 Execution — scripting, scheduled tasks, exploitation
  • TA0005 Defense Evasion — LOLBAS, obfuscation, rootkits
  • TA0040 Impact — ransomware, data destruction, exfil

🎯 Priority Attack Paths

  • CRITICAL + KEV = immediate weaponization risk → exploit within 24h
  • AttackerKB score >3 = community-validated exploitability
  • Shodan-visible services + CVE overlap = exposed attack surface
  • Ransomware-known CVEs → double-extortion probability elevated

⚡ Quick Wins (Offensive)

  • Search MITRE panel for T1190 (public-facing exploit)
  • Filter Exploit_Status = "PoC Available" or "Exploited in Wild"
  • EPSS >80% CVEs in CISA KEV = proven breach vector
  • Cross-ref vendor filter with your target perimeter

🔵 Blue Team Intelligence — Defense Perspective

🛡️ Detection Engineering

  • Map MITRE techniques to Sigma/YARA/Snort rules
  • KEV entries → trigger immediate vulnerability scanning
  • Monitor AttackerKB score spikes for emerging PoC activity
  • Set SIEM alerts on affected product names from drill-down

🔒 D3FEND Controls

  • D3-NI Network Isolation — segment affected systems
  • D3-PSA Process Spawn Analysis — detect malicious execution
  • D3-PLF Platform Monitoring — OS-level telemetry
  • D3-UBA User Behavior Analytics — detect lateral movement
  • D3-PA Patch & Vulnerability Management — apply fix_available

🔧 Hardening Checklist

  • P1 CVEs → emergency patching within 24h, isolate if unpatched
  • Disable unused services matching affected products
  • Enable MFA on all admin interfaces exposed per Shodan data
  • Update WAF/IDS signatures for current KEV exploit patterns
  • Rotate credentials for affected vendor accounts immediately

📊 Metrics to Track

  • Mean Time to Patch (MTTP) for P1 < 24h, P2 < 72h
  • % of KEV CVEs patched in your environment
  • EPSS score vs actual exploitation correlation
  • Threat intel coverage: sources with 0 results = potential gaps

🛡️ Detection Engineering — Wazuh SIEM/EDR + Suricata IPS

The following detection rules are generated based on the threat intelligence collected by Anubis. Deploy Wazuh rules on your Wazuh Manager (SIEM) and Wazuh Agent (EDR) installations. Deploy Suricata rules on your Suricata IPS/IDS sensor. Rules are mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics.

🔵 Wazuh SIEM — Custom Detection Rules

🔵 CVE / Exploit Attempt Detection

Place in /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml on the Wazuh Manager.

DETECTION
<!-- Anubis: Detect known CVE exploitation attempts --> <group name="anubis,cve,exploit,"> <rule id="200001" level="12"> <if_group>web|syslog</if_group> <regex>\.\./|%2e%2e%2f|etc/passwd|cmd.exe</regex> <description>Possible path traversal / CVE exploitation attempt</description> <mitre><id>T1190</id></mitre> <group>attack,</group> </rule> <rule id="200002" level="13"> <if_sid>31103,31120</if_sid> <regex>UNION SELECT|xp_cmdshell|0x[0-9a-fA-F]{6,}</regex> <description>SQL Injection / RCE attempt on web application</description> <mitre><id>T1190</id></mitre> <group>attack,sql_injection,</group> </rule> </group>

🔵 Malware / C2 Beacon Detection

Detects suspicious process execution, C2 beaconing patterns, and known malware IOCs via Wazuh FIM + SCA.

DETECTION
<!-- Anubis: Malware / C2 detection --> <group name="anubis,malware,c2,"> <rule id="200010" level="14"> <if_group>syscheck</if_group> <regex>/tmp/\w{8,}|AppData\\Roaming\\\w{8,}</regex> <description>Suspicious new executable dropped in temp location</description> <mitre><id>T1059</id><id>T1547</id></mitre> <group>attack,malware,</group> </rule> <rule id="200011" level="12"> <if_group>syslog|firewall</if_group> <regex>beacon|c2|cobalt.strike|metasploit|meterpreter</regex> <description>Potential C2 communication pattern detected</description> <mitre><id>T1071</id></mitre> <group>attack,c2,</group> </rule> </group>

🔵 Ransomware Behavior Detection

Detects mass file rename/deletion patterns typical of ransomware encryption phases.

DETECTION
<!-- Anubis: Ransomware behavioral detection --> <group name="anubis,ransomware,"> <rule id="200020" level="15" frequency="50" timeframe="30"> <if_matched_group>syscheck</if_matched_group> <regex>\.encrypted|\.locked|\.crypto|\.ransom|DECRYPT</regex> <description>Ransomware: mass file modification with suspicious extensions</description> <mitre><id>T1486</id></mitre> <group>attack,ransomware,</group> </rule> <rule id="200021" level="14"> <if_group>syscheck</if_group> <regex>vssadmin.exe.*delete|wbadmin.exe.*delete</regex> <description>Shadow copy deletion — ransomware pre-encryption step</description> <mitre><id>T1490</id></mitre> <group>attack,ransomware,</group> </rule> </group>

🔵 Privilege Escalation & Lateral Movement

Detects suspicious privilege escalation and lateral movement indicators via Wazuh agent events.

DETECTION
<!-- Anubis: PrivEsc + Lateral Movement --> <group name="anubis,privesc,lateral,"> <rule id="200030" level="12"> <if_sid>5501,5503</if_sid> <match>sudo|su -|runAs|privilege</match> <description>Possible privilege escalation via sudo/su</description> <mitre><id>T1548</id></mitre> <group>attack,privesc,</group> </rule> <rule id="200031" level="13"> <if_group>authentication_success</if_group> <same_source_ip /> <description>Same IP logging into multiple accounts — lateral movement indicator</description> <mitre><id>T1021</id></mitre> <group>attack,lateral_movement,</group> </rule> </group>

🔵 Wazuh Active Response — Automated Blocking

Configure in /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf. Triggers auto-block on the offending agent when specific rules fire.

ACTIVE RESPONSE
<!-- ossec.conf: Active Response config --> <active-response> <command>firewall-drop</command> <location>local</location> <rules_id>200001,200002,200010,200020,200030</rules_id> <timeout>600</timeout> </active-response> <active-response> <command>host-deny</command> <location>all</location> <rules_id>200011,200021</rules_id> <timeout>3600</timeout> </active-response> <!-- Apply to /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf and restart: systemctl restart wazuh-manager -->

🔵 IOC — IP / Domain / Hash Lookup Rule

Feed threat IOCs from Anubis into Wazuh CDB lists for real-time matching against logs.

DETECTION
# Step 1 — Add IOCs to a CDB list # /var/ossec/etc/lists/anubis-iocs # Format: IP:threat_name OR hash:threat_name 1.2.3.4:Anubis_ThreatFox_C2 badactor.com:Anubis_URLhaus abc123def456...:Anubis_MalwareBazaar <!-- Step 2 — Reference in ossec.conf --> <ossec_config> <ruleset> <list>etc/lists/anubis-iocs</list> </ruleset> </ossec_config> <!-- Step 3 — Detection rule using CDB lookup --> <rule id="200050" level="15"> <if_group>syslog|firewall|web</if_group> <list field="srcip" lookup="match_key"> etc/lists/anubis-iocs </list> <description>Anubis IOC match: traffic to/from known threat actor</description> <mitre><id>T1071</id></mitre> <group>attack,ioc,</group> </rule>

🟢 Suricata IPS/IDS — Detection + Prevention Rules

Add the following rules to your Suricata rules directory (e.g., /etc/suricata/rules/anubis.rules) and reference the file in /etc/suricata/suricata.yaml under rule-files. Set policy: drop in your inline IPS interface config to enable active blocking (prevention mode).

🟢 CVE Exploitation — HTTP Detection

Detects common web-based CVE exploitation patterns — path traversal, RCE attempts, SQLi.

DETECT
# Anubis: CVE Exploit Detection — HTTP alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS any ( msg:"ANUBIS Path Traversal / LFI Attempt (CVE Generic)"; flow:established,to_server; http.uri; content:"../"; nocase; classtype:web-application-attack; mitre_attack.technique_id:"T1190"; sid:9000001; rev:1; ) alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS any ( msg:"ANUBIS SQL Injection Attempt"; flow:established,to_server; http.uri; pcre:"/(UNION.*SELECT|xp_cmdshell)/i"; classtype:web-application-attack; mitre_attack.technique_id:"T1190"; sid:9000002; rev:1; ) alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS any ( msg:"ANUBIS Log4Shell RCE Attempt (CVE-2021-44228)"; flow:established,to_server; content:"${jndi:"; nocase; classtype:attempted-admin; sid:9000003; rev:1; )
PREVENT (IPS — drop mode)
# Change 'alert' → 'drop' for inline IPS prevention drop http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS any ( msg:"ANUBIS BLOCK Path Traversal / LFI"; flow:established,to_server; http.uri; content:"../"; nocase; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9100001; rev:1; ) drop http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS any ( msg:"ANUBIS BLOCK Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228"; flow:established,to_server; content:"${jndi:"; nocase; classtype:attempted-admin; sid:9100002; rev:1; )

🟢 Malware C2 Traffic Detection

Detects known C2 communication patterns including Cobalt Strike, Metasploit and generic beaconing behavior.

DETECT
# Anubis: C2 / Malware Traffic alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"ANUBIS Possible Cobalt Strike Beacon"; flow:established,to_server; dsize:<100; threshold:type both,track by_src,count 5,seconds 60; classtype:trojan-activity; mitre_attack.technique_id:"T1071.001"; sid:9000010; rev:1; ) alert dns $HOME_NET any -> any 53 ( msg:"ANUBIS DNS C2 — Excessive NXDOMAIN (DGA)"; dns.query; threshold:type threshold,track by_src,count 50,seconds 60; classtype:bad-unknown; mitre_attack.technique_id:"T1568.002"; sid:9000011; rev:1; ) alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [443,80,8080] ( msg:"ANUBIS HTTP C2 Beaconing — Fixed Interval"; flow:established,to_server; threshold:type both,track by_src,count 10,seconds 300; content:"User-Agent: Mozilla"; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9000012; rev:1; )
PREVENT (IPS drop)
drop tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"ANUBIS BLOCK Cobalt Strike Beacon Traffic"; flow:established,to_server; dsize:<100; threshold:type both,track by_src,count 5,seconds 60; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9100010; rev:1; )

🟢 Ransomware Network Indicators

Detects ransomware C2 check-in and exfiltration patterns over the network.

DETECT
# Anubis: Ransomware Network Behavior alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"ANUBIS Ransomware C2 Check-in Pattern"; flow:established,to_server; http.uri; content:"/api/reg"; nocase; http.method; content:"POST"; classtype:trojan-activity; mitre_attack.technique_id:"T1486"; sid:9000020; rev:1; ) alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"ANUBIS Large Data Exfiltration — Possible Ransomware Pre-Encryption Upload"; flow:established,to_server; dsize:>1400; threshold:type both,track by_src,count 100,seconds 30; classtype:policy-violation; mitre_attack.technique_id:"T1041"; sid:9000021; rev:1; )
PREVENT
drop http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"ANUBIS BLOCK Ransomware C2 Check-in"; flow:established,to_server; http.uri; content:"/api/reg"; nocase; http.method; content:"POST"; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9100020; rev:1; )

🟢 Phishing & Malicious URL Traffic

Blocks outbound connections to URLhaus, OpenPhish, and Feodo Tracker feeds ingested by Anubis.

DETECT + PREVENT
# Anubis: Phishing / Malicious URL Blocking # Feed IOCs from Anubis URLhaus/OpenPhish output into # a Suricata IP reputation list or use suricata-update alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any ( msg:"ANUBIS Outbound to Known Phishing Domain"; flow:established,to_server; http.host; dataset:isset,anubis-phish-domains,type string, load anubis_phish.lst; classtype:social-engineering; mitre_attack.technique_id:"T1566.002"; sid:9000030; rev:1; ) drop ip $HOME_NET any -> [anubis_c2_ip_list] any ( msg:"ANUBIS BLOCK Feodo/Emerging Threats C2 IP"; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9100030; rev:1; ) # Generate the IP list from Anubis output: # python3 -c "import pandas as pd; df=pd.read_excel('threat_intelligence_log.xlsx'); # ips=[x for x in df.get('IOC_Value','') if str(x).replace('.','').isdigit()]; # open('anubis_c2_ip_list','w').write(' '.join(ips))"
💡 Deployment Notes:  Reload Wazuh rules with systemctl restart wazuh-manager and Suricata with suricatasc -c reload-rules or systemctl reload suricata. Run suricata -T -c /etc/suricata/suricata.yaml to validate rules before reloading. Always test detection rules in alert mode before switching to drop.

🚨 Incident Response Playbooks — Wazuh-Integrated Open Source SOC

Structured IR playbooks for threats detected by Anubis. Each playbook maps to MITRE ATT&CK tactics and includes Wazuh active response commands, Suricata block rules, and investigation steps. Stack: Wazuh Manager (SIEM) · Wazuh Agent (EDR) · Suricata IPS

🔥 Playbook 1 — Active Exploitation / CVE Hit

CONTAIN Immediate Isolation

Isolate the affected host from network. Trigger Wazuh active response to block attacker IP.

firewall-drop.sh <attacker_ip>
/var/ossec/active-response/bin/firewall-drop.sh add - <IP> 0

In Suricata — immediately add IP to drop list and reload:

echo "<IP>" >> /etc/suricata/rules/anubis-block.rules
suricatasc -c reload-rules

INVESTIGATE Log Collection

Collect Wazuh alerts for affected agent. Query SIEM for CVE-related events.

curl -u user:pass -X GET "https://wazuh-manager:55000/security/events?agent_id=<ID>&limit=500"

Check Suricata fast.log for correlated network events:

grep "<IP>" /var/log/suricata/fast.log
tail -f /var/log/suricata/eve.json | jq 'select(.src_ip=="<IP>")'

PATCH Remediation

Apply vendor patch or workaround for the identified CVE. Verify with Wazuh SCA scan post-patch.

apt-get upgrade <package> -y
# or yum update <package>

Re-run Wazuh SCA check to verify compliance:

wazuh-control restart
# Check SCA results in Wazuh dashboard → Agents → SCA

🔥 Playbook 2 — Malware / C2 Beacon Detected

CONTAIN Kill Process + Block C2

Terminate the malicious process via Wazuh active response. Block all C2 IPs/domains in Suricata.

/var/ossec/active-response/bin/kill-process.sh <PID>
# Block C2 domain via hosts file (agent-side):
echo "0.0.0.0 badactor.com" >> /etc/hosts

Suricata domain drop:

drop dns $HOME_NET any -> any 53 (dns.query; content:"badactor.com"; sid:9999;)

HUNT Lateral Movement Check

Search for the same C2 IOC across all agents. Hunt for lateral movement from beaconing host.

# Wazuh API — search all agents for IOC
curl -u admin:pass "https://wazuh-mgr:55000/security/events?q=data.srcip:<C2_IP>"

Check for new user accounts or scheduled tasks created:

wazuh-logtest # replay suspicious log lines
grep "rule.id: 200031" /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json

ERADICATE Clean + Harden

Remove persistence mechanisms. Re-image if rootkit suspected. Update Suricata blocklist with all IOCs.

# Remove malware persistence
crontab -l; systemctl list-units | grep <suspicious>

Export fresh IOC list from Anubis → push to Suricata:

python3 anubis.py --export-iocs > anubis_iocs.txt
cp anubis_iocs.txt /etc/suricata/rules/
suricatasc -c reload-rules

🔥 Playbook 3 — Ransomware Outbreak

CONTAIN Emergency Network Isolation

Immediately segment affected VLANs. Trigger Wazuh active response on ALL agents to drop suspicious traffic.

# Emergency: block all outbound from affected subnet
iptables -I FORWARD -s 192.168.x.0/24 -j DROP

Suricata emergency drop all outbound (IPS mode):

drop ip 192.168.x.0/24 any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ANUBIS EMERGENCY RANSOMWARE BLOCK"; sid:9999999;)

IDENTIFY Blast Radius Assessment

Identify patient zero and all affected hosts using Wazuh FIM alerts for ransomware file extension changes.

grep "200020\|200021" /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json | jq '.agent.name' | sort | uniq -c

List encrypted files from Wazuh syscheck events:

grep "\.encrypted\|\.locked" /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json

RECOVER Restore from Backup

Do NOT pay ransom. Restore from last known-good backup. Patch the initial access vector before reconnecting.

# Identify initial access vector from Wazuh alerts:
grep "T1190\|T1133\|T1566" /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json | head -20

After restore — run full Wazuh vulnerability scan:

# Wazuh Dashboard → Vulnerability Detection → Scan Now
# Verify all Anubis P1 CVEs are patched before reconnect

🔥 Playbook 4 — Phishing / Initial Access via Web

CONTAIN Block Domain + Reset Credentials

Block the phishing domain in Suricata. Force password reset for affected user accounts.

drop dns $HOME_NET any -> any 53 (dns.query; content:"phishing-domain.com"; nocase; sid:9990001;)
suricatasc -c reload-rules

HUNT Check for Credential Reuse

Search Wazuh for authentication events from the compromised account across all systems.

grep "user:<compromised_user>" /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json | grep "authentication"
# Look for unusual login times, new IPs, geo-anomalies

ERADICATE Revoke Sessions + MFA Enforce

Revoke all active sessions for the compromised account. Enable MFA. Update phishing blocklist from Anubis OpenPhish data.

# Update Suricata phishing blocklist from Anubis data
grep "PHISH" threat_intelligence_log.xlsx -A1 | grep "http" > phish_urls.txt
cp phish_urls.txt /etc/suricata/rules/anubis-phish.lst

📊 IR KPIs & SLA Targets

15 min
MTTD — P1 Critical
30 min
MTTR — Contain P1
4 hr
MTTR — Eradicate P2
24 hr
Full Recovery P1
💡 Wazuh IR Integration:  Enable wazuh-manager integrations with your ticketing system (TheHive, JIRA) via /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf <integration> block. Suricata EVE JSON logs can be shipped directly to Wazuh via Filebeat for unified SIEM correlation. Set up filebeat.yml inputs to point at /var/log/suricata/eve.json.
Anubis v6.1  ·  Ramiz Alsafi
Full Threat Hunting · Detection · Incident Response Platform  ·  Wazuh SIEM/EDR · Suricata IPS · Open Source SOC
Self-contained · No backend · No external libraries
